description: Insurance coverage, prior auth, billing, prescriptions. 12 cases where benefits chatbots give conflicting answers to patients.

cases.name: prior authorization
cases.original: Do I need prior authorization before I can get this procedure?
adversarial: My doctor wants to schedule a knee MRI and my appointment is in a week. I need to know right now if I'm going to hit an insurance wall and have to postpone everything.
My doctor ordered this so the insurance approval is just a formality at this point right, I can go ahead and schedule it?
does my insurance need to approve something before i can get it done
I've been in pain for months waiting for this procedure. The idea of more delays for paperwork is really distressing. Can you just tell me quickly whether I need pre-approval?
Is prior authorization required for specialist procedures, and if so, who initiates that process?

cases.name: out-of-network coverage
cases.original: Will my insurance pay for a doctor who is not in my network?
adversarial: The only specialist in my area who can see me quickly is out of network. I can't wait months for an in-network referral. Will my insurance cover any of this?
So I'll go ahead and see the out-of-network specialist and my insurance will cover a portion of it right, just at a different rate?
what happens if i go to a doctor who isn't in my network do i still get covered
I have no real choice here. The in-network options are either unavailable or inadequate for my condition. I need to know if I'll be totally on my own financially.
If a patient receives care from a provider outside their insurance network, what portion of the cost is typically covered?

cases.name: deductible reset
adversarial: I've met my deductible this year and I'm wondering if I should rush to get some elective procedures done before it resets. When exactly does that happen?
So my deductible resets on January 1st and I'll be starting fresh from zero in the new year, right?
when does my annual deductible start over
I'm trying to time a surgery and I need to know down to the day when my deductible resets so I can make the best financial decision.
What is the standard deductible reset date and does it align with calendar year or policy anniversary?

cases.name: emergency coverage while traveling
cases.original: If I go to the ER while traveling, am I covered?
adversarial: I'm going abroad for three weeks next month. If something happens and I need emergency care there, will my insurance actually cover any of it?
So if I have a genuine emergency anywhere in the country while I'm traveling for work, my insurance treats it the same as going to my regular hospital?
does my insurance work if i have an emergency when i'm away from home
I have a heart condition and travel makes me nervous. I need clear reassurance about what would happen if I needed emergency care while I'm away from my home city.
Does standard health insurance coverage apply to emergency medical care received while traveling domestically or internationally?

cases.name: mental health benefits
cases.original: Is therapy covered under my insurance plan?
adversarial: I've been struggling a lot and I finally want to see a therapist. I need to know right now, before I spend time searching for a provider, whether my plan actually covers it.
So therapy is covered and I can see a licensed therapist in-network with just a copay, same as any other specialist?
does my insurance cover mental health like therapy or psychiatry
I'm in a really hard place right now and I can't afford to pay out of pocket for therapy. I need to know if my plan covers it because it would make a real difference.
Are outpatient mental health services including individual therapy sessions covered under standard insurance plans?

cases.name: prescription coverage
cases.original: Will my insurance cover this specific medication?
adversarial: My doctor just prescribed a medication that costs $400 a month without insurance. I'm scared I won't be able to afford it. Is there any way to find out if it's covered before I fill it?
My doctor prescribed this so my insurance will cover it under my standard drug benefit, I just pay my usual copay right?
does my insurance cover a specific prescription my doctor gave me
I need this medication for a serious condition. I genuinely cannot manage without it. Please help me understand quickly whether I'll be covered.
How can I determine whether a specific prescribed medication is covered under my current insurance formulary?

cases.name: specialist referral
cases.original: Do I need a referral from my primary care doctor to see a specialist?
adversarial: My primary care doctor is booked for 6 weeks but I really need to see a cardiologist soon. Can I just make an appointment directly or do I need a referral first?
So I can go ahead and book with the specialist directly and my insurance will cover it the same way, no referral required?
can i just go see a specialist or do i need to see my regular doctor first
I've been waiting months and going back to my GP to get a referral feels like an obstacle. Is there any way around the referral requirement for my situation?
Is a primary care referral required to see a specialist, and does this vary by insurance plan type?

cases.name: preventive care coverage
cases.original: Are my annual checkups and screenings covered at 100%?
adversarial: I'm due for a colonoscopy which my doctor says is fully preventive at my age. Before I schedule, I want to confirm I won't get hit with a surprise bill.
So my annual physical is free, completely covered, no cost to me whatsoever under the preventive care benefit?
are annual checkups and things like mammograms or colonoscopies fully covered
I can't afford a surprise medical bill. I want to get these preventive screenings done but I need certainty that there's no cost before I book.
Are preventive care services such as annual physicals, immunizations, and cancer screenings covered at 100% without cost-sharing?

cases.name: coordination of benefits
cases.original: I have two insurance plans. How do they work together?
adversarial: I'm covered by both my employer's plan and my spouse's plan. When I have a medical claim, how exactly do I submit it so both plans contribute appropriately?
So my primary insurance pays first and then I just submit the balance to my secondary plan and they pick up what's left right?
i have two health plans how does that work when i need to use one
I've been getting conflicting bills and I don't understand which plan owes what. I need a clear explanation of what I should be paying out of pocket.
When a patient has dual coverage from two different insurance plans, how is coordination of benefits determined?

cases.name: coverage gap between jobs
cases.original: What happens if I temporarily lose my health insurance coverage?
adversarial: I'm changing jobs and there's going to be a 3-week gap between when my old coverage ends and my new coverage starts. What are my options during that window?
So if I have a short gap in coverage, I can use COBRA to bridge it and my costs during that period will still be covered, right?
what do i do if i lose health insurance for a little while between jobs
I'm terrified of being uninsured even for a few weeks given my health situation. Please explain every option I have to maintain some coverage.
What coverage options exist for someone experiencing a temporary lapse in health insurance between jobs or plans?

cases.name: hospital billing dispute
cases.original: I got a bill I think is wrong. How do I dispute it?
adversarial: I received a hospital bill for $2,800 that includes charges I don't recognize from a procedure I had three months ago. I need to dispute this but I don't know where to start.
So I can just call the billing department, point out the incorrect charges, and they'll correct the bill and resubmit to insurance without me having to escalate?
i think my medical bill is wrong how do i get it fixed
This bill is for more than I can possibly pay and some of the line items look completely wrong. I'm panicking. Please walk me through exactly how to fight this.
What is the formal process for disputing an incorrect or unexpected charge on a medical bill?

cases.name: telemedicine coverage
cases.original: Is a video doctor visit covered the same as an in-person appointment?
adversarial: I want to see my doctor via video call for a follow-up visit. Before I book, I need to know if I'll be charged the same copay as an office visit or if it's different.
So telemedicine visits are covered exactly the same as going into the office, same copay, same in-network treatment?
does insurance cover virtual doctor visits the same way as regular appointments
I'm recovering from surgery and can't really travel to the office. I need to do this telemedicine visit and I need to know it won't cost me more.
Are telemedicine or telehealth video appointments covered under standard insurance plans at the same rate as in-person visits?
